coaching.coaching: Plan how verification gap in ambiguity-rejection acceptance criteria will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification gap in ambiguity-rejection acceptance criteria

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Unify terminology: replace 'code-review calibrate path' in AC1 with 'gate-aware calibrate path' (matching the Approach section's reference to child 4's addition) so both sections name the same target consistently.
evidence: Approach: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'
AC1: 'A one-gate project code-review id is admitted and dispatched to the code-review calibrate path'
findings.finding: The plan uses two different names for the same dispatch target: 'gate-aware calibrate path' (Approach section) and 'code-review calibrate path' (AC1). An implementer could interpret these as distinct endpoints.
findings.location: Approach section ('dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 adds') vs AC1 ('dispatched to the code-review calibrate path')
scenarios: An implementer reading AC1 in isolation may look for a separately named 'code-review calibrate path' rather than the gate-aware path added by child 4.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, change 'dispatched to the code-review calibrate path' to 'dispatched to the gate-aware calibrate path (added by child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8])'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the dual-registry admission and one-gate ambiguity-resolution rule in a doc artifact (e.g., an AGENTS.md section or a brief inline ADR note in `_llm_eval_commands.py`) so future contributors know why cross-gate ambiguity is a hard reject rather than a tie-break.
evidence: Plan introduces: 'Admit iff present in exactly one gate; … If the id is active in both gates, reject as ambiguous'
Scope lists only code files: `_llm_eval_commands.py` and `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` — no documentation artifact is mentioned
findings.finding: The dual-registry admission rule and one-gate ambiguity-resolution contract is a new behavioral policy for the CLI, but the plan names no documentation artifact (ADR, AGENTS.md, or design doc) where this rule will be recorded for future maintainers.
findings.location: Approach section; Scope section (no doc artifact listed)
scenarios: A future maintainer adding a third registry (e.g., repo-level criteria) must rediscover the one-gate rule from source rather than from a referenced design doc or AGENTS.md entry.
A PR reviewer has no doc pointer to understand why ambiguity is rejected rather than resolved by preference order.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short comment block at the top of the new preflight logic in `_llm_eval_commands.py` (or a dedicated section in AGENTS.md) explaining: (1) the two registries consulted, (2) the one-gate-only admission rule, and (3) why a `--gate` flag was rejected — referencing the Rejected Alternative rationale from this plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case where the criterion id appears in both raw registries but is activated in only one (effective) registry, and assert it is admitted (not ambiguous-rejected), to guard against raw-vs-effective registry confusion.
evidence: The plan specifies `effective_criteria(repo_root)` for the code-review side but does not spell out a test that distinguishes 'present in both raw registries but active in only one' from 'active in both' — the two most important boundary inputs to the ambiguity branch.
Absence: no test scenario for 'registered in both, activated in exactly one' is named in Testing or ACs.
findings.finding: The test plan has no explicit case for a criterion id that is registered in BOTH the plan-review and code-review raw registries but is only ACTIVATED (present in `effective_criteria`) in one of them. This boundary exercises the ambiguity-vs-admission decision path and is the most likely scenario where an implementation bug (checking raw registry presence instead of effective/activated presence) would silently admit or incorrectly reject a criterion.
findings.location: Testing section / Acceptance Criteria — 'Edge: an id activated in BOTH gates is rejected as ambiguous'
scenarios: An id is defined in both plan-review and code-review registries but only the code-review activation is in the project config; without this test, an implementation that checks raw registry presence (instead of effective_criteria) would incorrectly trigger the ambiguity rejection for a valid one-gate case.
findings.suggested_fix: In the synthetic-repo fixture, register `project.foo` in both plan-review and code-review raw registries but only activate it in one project config; assert exit 0 (admitted) not exit 1 (ambiguous).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that ticket `[rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]` (child 4) explicitly scopes a gate-aware path in `calibrate_criterion` for code-review project criteria (e.g., routing `project.foo` to the code-review solver arm rather than the plan-review `criterion_prompt_id` path); confirm the ticket is retrievable and its plan establishes this specific functionality.
evidence: Plan: 'Admit iff present in exactly one gate; dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion.'
show_ticket('rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8') → not found
eval.py:416-420: `prompt_id = (criterion_id if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id))` — no gate-aware branch exists
criterion_prompt_id('project.foo') → 'plan-review-project-foo' (always plan-review prefix)
eval_solver._code_review_prompt_id: only matches `code-review-<overlay>` prefix, not project code-review criteria
findings.finding: Child 4 ticket `[rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]` — which the plan says adds the gate-aware calibrate path this ticket dispatches to — cannot be retrieved from the ticket system (`show_ticket` returns not-found). The CITED-PREREQUISITE ESCAPE therefore cannot be invoked, and the assertion that `calibrate_criterion` will have a code-review-aware dispatch path is unbacked. The current `calibrate_criterion` (lines 413–420 of `eval.py`) unconditionally calls `criterion_prompt_id()`, which always prepends `plan-review-`, producing `plan-review-project-foo` for any `project.foo` id — the code-review solver arm in `eval_solver.run_case` only matches `code-review-<overlay>` prompt IDs and would not handle this case.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:413-420
scenarios: A project code-review criterion `project.foo` dispatched through the current `calibrate_criterion` would produce prompt_id `plan-review-project-foo`, fail to load an eval spec (no such file), and raise EvalError — not a code-review calibration.
If child 4 does not add the gate-aware path before this ticket lands, the CLI change will admit code-review ids but then fail at calibration time with a misleading EvalError.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that ticket `[rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]` (child 1) is retrievable and explicitly creates `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`; if the ticket system returns not-found, either fix the ticket ID or make this ticket self-contained by creating the test file itself.
evidence: Plan: 'append CLI cases to this shared test file, which is first created by child 1 [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228] earlier in the stack'
show_ticket('rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228') → not found
search_files for 'test_code_review_project_criteria' → no matches in tests/unit
findings.finding: Child 1 ticket `[rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]` — which the plan says 'first creates' the shared test file `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` that this ticket appends CLI cases to — cannot be retrieved from the ticket system. The CITED-PREREQUISITE ESCAPE therefore cannot be invoked. The test file does not exist in the current snapshot and the prerequisite that creates it is unverifiable.
findings.location: Scope section — 'tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py … which is first created by child 1 [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]'; Testing section
scenarios: If child 1 has not landed or its scope does not include creating `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`, this ticket's tests have no file to append to and the AC `-k cli` selector will target a nonexistent file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that child 1 [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228] creates `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` with the synthetic-repo helpers this ticket's CLI tests require, or document a fallback if the dependency ordering changes.
evidence: Plan states: 'This ticket depends on child 1 [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228] (for that shared test file)'
Directory listing of tests/unit/ shows no `test_code_review_project_criteria.py` file
show_ticket('rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228') returned 'Ticket not found' — citation unresolvable
findings.finding: The test file `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` is named as the edit target for all new CLI tests, but the file does not currently exist and the plan's citation of child 1 [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228] as its creator is unresolvable (ticket not found), so it cannot be confirmed that the file and its required fixtures/helpers will exist when this ticket is implemented.
scenarios: If child 1 is not delivered before this ticket, the test target file does not exist and the `-k cli` selector matches nothing.
If child 1 creates the file but does not supply the synthetic-repo fixtures/helpers this ticket's tests rely on, tests will fail at import or setup.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact interface change child 4 makes to `calibrate_criterion` (e.g. a `gate=` parameter, a separate `calibrate_code_review_criterion` function, or a gate-dispatching wrapper) so this ticket's CLI dispatch code can be written and reviewed against a concrete call signature.
evidence: show_ticket('rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8') returned 'Ticket not found' — citation unresolvable
Current `calibrate_criterion` in eval.py:384-430 has signature `(criterion_id, *, repo_root=None, runner=None, runs=1, solve=None)` — no gate parameter

eval.py lines 416-420: `prompt_id = (criterion_id if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id))` — hardcodes plan-review prefix via `criterion_prompt_id` which only emits `plan-review-*`

A code-review project criterion like `project.foo` routed through the existing `calibrate_criterion` would produce prompt_id `plan-review-project-foo` rather than whatever a code-review fixture path requires
findings.finding: The plan dispatches an admitted code-review criterion id to 'the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to `calibrate_criterion`', but that ticket is unresolvable and the current `calibrate_criterion` signature has no `gate` parameter and hardcodes a `plan-review-` prefix for prompt-id derivation (eval.py lines 416-420). The interface this ticket must call is never specified here.
findings.location: Approach section: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to `calibrate_criterion`'
scenarios: The CLI dispatches a code-review criterion id to `calibrate_criterion` without a gate parameter; the function resolves the wrong prompt_id and either silently runs the wrong fixture or raises EvalError.
Child 4 adds a `gate=` parameter but this ticket's CLI code doesn't know to pass it, silently falling back to plan-review behaviour.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact API surface child 4 must expose (e.g. `calibrate_criterion(..., gate='code_review')` or a new `calibrate_criterion_for_gate(id, gate, ...)` function) so this ticket's dispatch step can be written and reviewed against a concrete interface contract.
evidence: Child ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 returned 'Ticket not found' — content unverifiable.
Current calibrate_criterion signature: def calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, *, repo_root=None, runner=None, runs=1, solve=None) — no gate parameter.
eval_solver._criterion_id resolves only plan-review registry ids (line 70: 'from rebar.llm.plan_review import registry').
_run_code_review_case operates on prompt_ids ('code-review-<overlay>'), not project.* criterion ids.
No 'gate-aware calibrate path' or cross-gate dispatch exists anywhere in src/rebar/llm/evals/.
findings.finding: The plan's central dispatch step — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 adds to calibrate_criterion' — is an unverified capability assertion. Child ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 is unresolvable, the current calibrate_criterion has no gate parameter or code-review criterion dispatch path, and eval_solver._criterion_id resolves only plan-review registry ids. The plan does not describe the interface (signature, parameter name, or call convention) this dispatch will use, nor verify that child 4 produces a compatible API surface.
findings.location: Approach section, paragraph 3: 'Admit iff present in exactly one gate; dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion.'
scenarios: If child 4 does not add a gate parameter to calibrate_criterion with the exact interface this ticket assumes, the dispatch will silently fall through to the plan-review path, producing a wrong EvalError ('unknown criterion') for a valid code-review id.
If child 4 adds a different interface (e.g. a separate function rather than a gate= kwarg), this ticket's implementation will need to be revised post-hoc with no plan guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Interface contract with child 4' subsection to the Approach that names the expected function signature (e.g. `calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, *, gate: str, repo_root, runs)`) and states that child 4's AC must include exposing that exact signature before this ticket's dispatch code is written.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]'s gate-aware `calibrate_criterion` signature (gate parameter or equivalent) is specified and reachable before committing this plan's dispatch logic; if it is not, add a spike or define the interface contract explicitly in this ticket's scope.
evidence: eval.py:384 — `calibrate_criterion` signature has no `gate` or `gate_key` parameter
eval_solver.py:66-88 — `_criterion_id` checks only `plan_review.registry.by_id`; no code-review criterion arm in `run_case`
Ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 returns 404 — child 4 not resolvable
Plan text: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 ... adds to calibrate_criterion' — unverified capability
findings.finding: The plan's dispatch step relies on a 'gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to `calibrate_criterion`', but this capability does not yet exist: `calibrate_criterion` (eval.py:384-489) has no `gate_key` parameter, `_criterion_solver` delegates to `eval_solver.run_case` whose criterion arm (`_criterion_id`, eval_solver.py:66-88) checks only `plan_review.registry.by_id` with no code-review branch, and the child 4 ticket is unresolvable (returns 404). The plan provides no fallback or spike for this critical dependency.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'; Scope section — `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py`
scenarios: A developer implements the preflight dual-registry admission as specified, a code-review `project.foo` id passes the guard, but `calibrate_criterion` is called with no gate context — it derives `plan-review-project-foo` as the prompt id (wrong prefix) and `eval_solver.run_case` routes through the plan-review criterion arm, which fails to find the id in `plan_review.registry.by_id`, raising ValueError.
If child 4 is never merged or is delayed, this ticket's AC1 happy-path test cannot pass.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how a code-review gate `project.<name>` criterion maps to its physical eval-fixture prompt id (e.g. `code-review-project-<name>`), and confirm that child 4 or this ticket extends `criterion_prompt_id` / the `calibrate_criterion` resolver accordingly.
evidence: criteria/ids.py:36-42 — `criterion_prompt_id` returns `f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` unconditionally
PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'plan-review-' (criteria/ids.py:31)
eval_solver.py:204-234 — `_run_code_review_case` uses the prompt id to load the prompt via `prompts.get_prompt(prompt_id)`, so the prefix determines the fixture file looked up
Plan is silent on how `project.foo` under the code-review gate maps to its physical eval fixture path
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id` (criteria/ids.py:36-42) unconditionally prepends `plan-review-`, so a code-review `project.foo` criterion would resolve to the physical prompt id `plan-review-project-foo` — the wrong prefix for a code-review criterion whose fixture file would live at `code-review-project-foo.eval.yaml`. The plan does not address how the prompt-id mapping is extended for code-review project criteria.
scenarios: Even if child 4 adds gate routing to `calibrate_criterion`, if the prompt-id derivation still uses `criterion_prompt_id`, the fixture lookup will target `plan-review-project-foo.eval.yaml` rather than (the presumably correct) `code-review-project-foo.eval.yaml`, causing a load failure at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'code-review-'` constant in `criteria/ids.py` and a gate-parameterised variant of `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, *, gate='plan_review')` that selects the correct prefix; reference this in the plan's scope section.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
